code:
password = request.form['password']
db.execute(f"INSERT INTO users (password) VALUES ('{password}')")

issues.message: Password stored in plain text. Use bcrypt or argon2 to hash passwords before storage.
SQL injection vulnerability. Use parameterized queries: db.execute('INSERT INTO users (password) VALUES (?)', [hashed_password])
API key hardcoded in source code. Move to environment variable: process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY
API key will be exposed in client-side code. Make API calls from backend instead.
SELECT * fetches all columns including potentially large fields. Specify only needed columns.
No LIMIT clause - will load all users into memory. Add pagination.
Memory leak: interval not cleaned up. Add return () => clearInterval(interval);
Polling every 1 second is aggressive. Consider WebSockets or longer intervals.
Can be simplified to list comprehension: return [item.transform() for item in items if item.status == 'active']
Bare except catches all exceptions including KeyboardInterrupt and SystemExit. Use 'except Exception:' at minimum.
Silently swallowing exceptions hides bugs. At minimum, log the error.
XSS vulnerability. User input rendered directly in HTML. Sanitize with a library like DOMPurify or use template escaping.
Mutating the database object. Use projection or create a new object: const { password, ...safeUser } = user; return safeUser;
Requests run sequentially. Use Promise.all to fetch in parallel.
Using innerHTML with untrusted content can enable XSS. Use textContent or sanitize input first.
Writing str(config) produces invalid JSON for dicts. Use json.dump(config, f) instead.

code:
const items = data.items.filter(item => item.active);
return items[0].name;

issues.message: items[0] may be undefined when no active items exist. Add an empty-state check before reading name.
